item.title: The Bone-Chilling Story Of Pamela Smart
item.description:
In 1990, Gregory Smart was found deceased in his Derry, New Hampshire, home. Investigators determined that his wife, Pamela Smart, had influenced a teenage acquaintance and his friends to carry out the crime. The case gained national attention as one of the first high-profile trials to be fully televised.
Pamela Smart was convicted of being an accomplice to first-degree murder, conspiracy to commit murder, and witness tampering. She was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole. While the perpetrators of the act have since been released, Smart remains incarcerated as she continues to maintain her innocence through various legal appeals.

media:content.media:title: The Bone-Chilling Story Of Pamela Smart
media:content.media:description:
In 1990, Gregory Smart was found deceased in his Derry, New Hampshire, home. Investigators determined that his wife, Pamela Smart, had influenced a teenage acquaintance and his friends to carry out the crime. The case gained national attention as one of the first high-profile trials to be fully televised.
Pamela Smart was convicted of being an accomplice to first-degree murder, conspiracy to commit murder, and witness tampering. She was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole. While the perpetrators of the act have since been released, Smart remains incarcerated as she continues to maintain her innocence through various legal appeals.

item.title: The Paper Towel Clue in the Gilgo Beach Case
item.description:
In the Gilgo Beach case, investigators uncovered a key forensic link tying Rex Heuermann to one of the victims. A paper towel recovered from victim Megan Waterman contained a distinct pattern that became central evidence.
Authorities later found a matching paper towel in Heuermann’s possession, along with purchase records showing he bought the same brand and pattern. Officials said the item was kept for years, strengthening the connection between him and the crime.
The evidence became a major breakthrough in a case that had remained unsolved for years. Prosecutors said it helped establish a direct link between the suspect and the victim, contributing to his eventual guilty plea in 2026.

media:content.media:title: The Paper Towel Clue in the Gilgo Beach Case
media:content.media:description:
In the Gilgo Beach case, investigators uncovered a key forensic link tying Rex Heuermann to one of the victims. A paper towel recovered from victim Megan Waterman contained a distinct pattern that became central evidence.
Authorities later found a matching paper towel in Heuermann’s possession, along with purchase records showing he bought the same brand and pattern. Officials said the item was kept for years, strengthening the connection between him and the crime.
The evidence became a major breakthrough in a case that had remained unsolved for years. Prosecutors said it helped establish a direct link between the suspect and the victim, contributing to his eventual guilty plea in 2026.
